title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for copy, replace, rename, delete, batch, and concurrency and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for conditional object-mutation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
Use validators, version IDs, idempotency records, or application locks to detect stale concurrent mutation and expose 409 or 412 meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Copy Replace Metadata · changed case · Quiz Copy Replace Metadata
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 2906 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Copy Replace Metadata without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Copy Replace Metadata · changed case · Quiz Copy Replace Metadata.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Batch Partial Failure, keep Batch Partial Failure connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Batch Partial Failure, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Batch Partial Failure precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Batch Partial Failure, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 8477 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Batch Partial Failure, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: One successful batch HTTP response means every requested object changed.
